content: We can frame a child's early self-directed learning and skill development as an innate biological equivalent of the LLM Self-Refine framework, where children iteratively refine their actions, language, and problem-solving approaches through unguided self-feedback rather than explicit adult supervision. This analogy suggests that studying how young children spontaneously generate self-critical feedback to improve their performance could inform the design of more efficient, robust Self-Refine systems for LLMs.
